ProfileSetup.description: First launch onboarding screen to capture user details.

PostLoad.description: Input form for drivers/operators to post load.
PostLoad.layout: Scrollable form with distinct grouped sections (Route, Cargo Details, Dimensions, Date).
elements: Cargo Allowed (Multi-select Chips: Bags, Carton, Pipes, Drums, Irregular)
Cargo Placement (Toggle/Segmented Control: Stackable vs Floor Only)

LoadMarket.layout: FlatList of Cards. Top section has simple filter (Origin/Dest Pincode input).
LoadMarket.cardData: Poster Name, Company, Phone, Origin, Dest, Cargo Type, Weight/Space, Date.
LoadMarket.actions: Prominent 'Call' button (Success color) on each card.

images.description: Subtle logistics background for profile setup if needed.

instructions_to_main_agent: Build this as an Expo React Native application using .js/.jsx files.
Use standard React Native components (View, Text, TextInput, TouchableOpacity, FlatList, ScrollView) and standard Expo icons (@expo/vector-icons).
All interactive elements MUST include a testID attribute (e.g., testID='post-load-submit-button').
Use standard RN StyleSheet.create() for styling based on the provided JSON specs.
Ensure large tap targets (minimum 44x44) for accessibility, especially for the non-tech-savvy driver demographic.
Navigation should be implemented using React Navigation (Bottom Tabs).
For the Cargo Placement toggle, use a row of two Touchables that act as a segmented control, highlighting the selected one.
Use a simple state management or AsyncStorage to handle the 'Profile setup screen (first launch)' logic.
Mock the data for the Load Market screen to demonstrate functionality.
Forms must have proper keyboard types (e.g., keyboardType='numeric' for pincodes, weight, space).
